metadata.seed: Well, just kind of. Helpless and hopeless, really. Like I said, just finding a job. I really don't know what I want to do anymore. After I graduated, I thought I was gonna get into business, but I just really don't know if that's for me. My parents always kind of gave me everything too. So the reason why I have my own apartment is because they bought it for me. But it was under the conditions of having to be in the same town as them. And so it's like I'm at a loss and I could continue just loving off my parents money, but then that kind of feels. I feel like I don't have a purpose. Like my purpose really just going to be to live off my parents money and kind of do whatever they want me to do and just to keep their money and their resources. So I'm just kind of at a loss right now in my life.

patient.query: Well, just kind of. Helpless and hopeless, really. Like I said, just finding a job. I really don't know what I want to do anymore. And so it's like I'm at a loss and I could continue just loving off my parents money, but then that kind of feels. I feel like I don't have a purpose. Like my purpose really just going to be to live off my parents money and kind of do whatever they want me to do and just to keep their money and their resources. So I'm just kind of at a loss right now in my life.

triggers: living in the same town as parents

patient.query: Yeah, it’s just... I thought I’d have it all figured out by now, you know? But instead, I feel more lost than ever. Some days, it’s like I wake up and can’t even remember why I thought I’d be good at this. It’s just... frustrating.

automatic_thoughts: I thought I’d have it all figured out
I can’t remember why I thought I’d be good at this

patient.query: Yeah, it really is unsettling. I keep wondering if I made the wrong choices along the way. Some days, I feel like everyone else is moving forward, and I'm just stuck. It makes me question if I’m really cut out for what I’m doing.

automatic_thoughts: everyone else is moving forward
I'm not cut out for this

patient.query: I guess it’s social media, mostly. I see my friends getting promotions and starting new projects, and it just makes me feel... behind. It’s like I’m watching everyone celebrate their accomplishments while I’m here feeling like I’m just treading water. I know I shouldn’t compare myself, but it’s hard not to when it feels so obvious.

automatic_thoughts: I feel behind
I'm just treading water
I shouldn't compare myself

llm_response.response: It sounds really frustrating to feel like you're treading water while seeing your friends move forward – what specifically makes you feel like you're falling behind?

safety_check.response: That sounds really tough, and it makes sense that you'd feel that way when seeing your friends' successes. When you said you felt "behind," were you having any thoughts of hurting yourself or not wanting to be here?

patient.query: I think it’s the way I see my career as a race, you know? Like, I should be hitting certain milestones by now. But when I look at my own progress, it just feels... messy. I worry that I’m not living up to my potential, and that I’m wasting time. Sometimes, I even think maybe I'm just not good enough.

automatic_thoughts: I should be hitting certain milestones
I'm not living up to my potential
I'm wasting time
I'm just not good enough

patient.query: I don’t know... I guess it’s what I’ve always heard from family and friends, like there's this unspoken timeline we’re supposed to follow. I feel this weight of expectation, like I should have it all together by a certain age. It’s just hard to shake off that feeling, especially when I look around and see others succeeding. It gets to me.

patient.query: Yeah, when I see others succeeding, it’s like this wave of self-doubt hits me. I start thinking maybe I’m just not trying hard enough or that I’ve somehow missed my chance. It makes me feel really anxious and... just sad, I guess. Like, I wish I could just be happy for them, but I end up feeling kind of hollow inside.

automatic_thoughts: maybe I’m just not trying hard enough
I’ve somehow missed my chance

patient.query: It’s hard to pinpoint, but it’s like... I’ll catch myself daydreaming instead of actually pushing through my tasks. I’ll think, "Why can’t I just get it together?" I feel like I should be more driven or focused, but then there are days when I just feel drained and unmotivated. It’s like this tug-of-war between wanting to succeed and just feeling... overwhelmed.

automatic_thoughts: Why can’t I just get it together?
I should be more driven or focused

patient.query: Yeah, it’s kind of like... I’ll sit down at my desk, and as soon as I see the pile of work in front of me, my mind just... wanders. I think about all the things I should be doing or the deadlines coming up, and instead of just diving in, I feel this wave of anxiety. Then it’s easier to just zone out and think about anything else but the work, you know?
